taboon-palestinian-flatbread.name: Taboon (palestinian Flatbread)
instructions: {'step': 'In a large mixing bowl, thoroughly stir together the bread flour, whole wheat flour, salt, and sugar.', 'number': '1'}

{'step': 'Make a well in the middle and add the oil, yeast, and water. Using your fingers, gradually stir the dry ingredients into the wet ones until a very soft and sticky ball of dough forms; if dough feels stiff, work in water 1 or 2 tablespoons (15-30ml) at a time until it is soft and slightly sticky.', 'number': '2'}

{'step': 'Let stand 5 minutes, then knead until the dough comes together more smoothly, even if still sticky, about 1 minute. Repeat this resting and kneading process once or twice more until a very soft and elastic ball of dough forms; when ready, the finished dough will still be a little sticky.', 'number': '3'}

{'step': 'Using well-oiled hands, gently shape the dough into a smooth ball, rub all over with olive oil, then set the dough ball in the bowl and cover with a damp tea towel or plastic wrap. Set aside at warm room temperature until dough doubles in size, about 90 minutes.', 'number': '4'}
{'step': 'Once the dough has risen, gently punch down to release the air bubbles.', 'number': '5'}
{'step': 'With well floured hands, divide into 8 equal portions, shape each into a ball, and place on a well-floured baking sheet or work surface.', 'number': '6'}
{'step': 'Cover with plastic wrap and set aside until dough balls grow by roughly 50%, about 30 minutes.', 'number': '7'}
{'step': 'If using optional “river” rocks (see recipe note), spread them in an even layer in a 10- or 12-inch cast iron skillet; otherwise proceed with empty skillet as directed.', 'number': '8'}
{'step': 'Adjust oven rack to middle position, set skillet on rack, and preheat to 500F° (260°C), or the highest the oven will go.', 'number': '9'}
{'step': 'Line a baking sheet with a clean kitchen towel.', 'number': '10'}
{'step': 'Working with one piece of dough at a time, set dough on a floured work surface, dust with more flour, then flatten slightly with your hand.', 'number': '11'}
{'step': 'Using your hands, flatten and stretch the dough out to a roughly 4-inch round.', 'number': '12'}
{'step': 'Flip it over, sprinkling with more flour if it feels sticky, and continue to flatten and stretch until you have a roughly 8-inch round.', 'number': '13'}

{'step': 'If using rocks, open the oven and pull out the rack halfway. Carefully transfer dough round directly to the skillet and lay it over the pebbles, carefully stretching it out to form a more circular shape.', 'number': '14'}

{'step': 'If you are not using pebbles, place dough round directly in the cast iron skillet and use your fingers to make indentations all over the bread (this will give it a similar shape and feel to having pebbles under it and also prevent it from rising and creating a pocket).', 'number': '15'}
{'step': 'Bake until the bread develops a very light golden top, about 5 minutes.', 'number': '16'}

{'step': 'Using oven mitts or silicone-tipped tongs, carefully lift taboon from the pebbles and, if the bottom is not brown, flip over and return to the oven until lightly golden on the second side, about 1 minute longer.', 'number': '17'}
{'step': 'Remove taboon from the oven and place on the towel-lined sheet to cool.', 'number': '18'}
{'step': 'Allow the rocks and/or skillet to reheat for 5 minutes as you stretch out the next piece of dough, then repeat the process until all the dough rounds are baked.', 'number': '19'}
{'step': 'Serve right away as desired or store in a zipper-lock bag for up to 1 day at room temperature or up to 1 month in the freezer.', 'number': '20'}

tacros-croissant-tacos.name: Tacros (croissant Tacos)
instructions: Combine masa harina and boiling water in the bowl of a stand mixer. Mix until crumbly and set aside to soften for a few minutes. Combine bread flour, gluten, sugar, salt, and yeast in a small bowl.

Pour milk into the bowl with the masa; add flour mixture and lard. Mix on low speed with the dough hook until dough is well combined and clears the sides of the bowl, about 2 minutes. Scrape dough down and mix for 5 minutes more to develop the gluten; dough will be very stiff.

Transfer dough to a lightly oiled bowl and cover with plastic wrap. Let rise at room temperature, about 1 1/2 hours. Place butter in the same mixing bowl and use the paddle attachment on low speed to break it up. Mix just until smooth.

Scrape butter onto a piece of parchment or waxed paper. Form into a 7-inch square. Cover with another piece of parchment and smooth the top with a rolling pin. Re-form the edges of your square if necessary; a little fussing now will save you some trouble later on as you roll out the dough. Place butter square in the refrigerator to chill.
Transfer dough onto a floured work surface. Pat it into a square, transfer to a parchment-lined baking sheet, cover with plastic wrap, and refrigerate for 1 to 24 hours.
Remove butter square from the refrigerator about 20 minutes before you plan to roll the dough; it needs to be cool and pliable.

Transfer dough onto a floured work surface. Roll it into a square a little larger than the butter square. Leaving a 7-inch square platform in the center for the butter, roll each of the 4 corners of dough out into elongated flaps that can wrap around the butter like a present.

Place butter square in the center of the dough, fold up the flaps, and pinch together to seal. Roll dough out into a rectangle. Fold in thirds, like a business letter. Wrap in plastic wrap and refrigerate for 30 minutes.

Return dough to the work surface; dust with flour if needed. Roll dough out into a longer rectangle; you're going to do a 4-fold, also called the 'book fold.' Fold the left edge of the dough into the center of the rectangle; fold the right side in to meet it. Then fold it in half again, like a wallet. Return dough to the parchment-lined baking pan, cover with plastic wrap, and refrigerate for 30 minutes.

Place dough on the work surface, roll out into a rectangle again, and make the final turn: another 3-fold, like a business letter. Cover and return to the refrigerator for 30 minutes; the dough can also be frozen at this point.

Slice dough in half and return 1 piece to the refrigerator to stay cool. Roll dough out to 1/4-inch thickness. Use a cutter to create 5-inch circles. Transfer circles to a parchment-lined baking sheet and let rise for 1 hour. Shape dough scraps into 'tacro cups' and place in the cups of a muffin tin; let rise. Repeat both steps with remaining dough.

Heat oil in a deep-fryer or large saucepan to 177°C (175 degrees C). Gently fold a dough circle in half to form a taco shell shape; holding it with tongs, lower it into the oil. Hold it closed for about 10 seconds. Fry 2 shells at a time until golden, turning once, about 3 minutes total. Repeat with remaining dough circles, maintaining the oil heat between 350 and 191°C (190 degrees C). Drain tacro shells on paper towels.
Preheat the oven to 191°C (190 degrees C).
Bake tacro cups in the muffin tin until golden, 15 to 18 minutes. Fill cups and shells with your favorite taco fillings.

tahini-carrot-winter-cake-recipe-lezzet.name: Tahini Carrot Winter Cake Recipe

tamagoyaki-japanese-egg-omelet.name: Tamagoyaki (japanese Egg Omelet)
instructions: In a small bowl, combine the dashi, salt, soy sauce, sugar, and water. Mix until everything is dissolved.
In a medium bowl, beat the eggs and add the seasoning mixture. Mix until well-combined.
Heat a tamagoyaki pan over medium-high heat. Brush a thin layer of oil on the pan.
Pour a third of the egg mixture into pan and quickly swirl to cover the entire pan. When the egg is half-set, gently roll the egg.

With the rolled egg still in the pan, pour in another third of the egg mixture. Lift up the rolled egg and let the mixture to flow under it. When the egg is half-set, roll the omelette toward you. Repeat with the rest of the egg mixture.
Slice into bite-sized pieces.
Enjoy!

tasmanian-ocean-trout-with-sauteed-mushr.name: Tasmanian Ocean Trout With Sautéed Mushrooms And Hollandaise Sauce

ingredients.item: -ounce skinless tasmanian ocean trout or salmon fillets
Hon shimeji mushrooms or oyster mushroom clusters

instructions: Whisk egg yolks, 2 tablespoons water, and lemon juice in metal bowl. Add butter. Place bowl over saucepan of barely simmering water; whisk constantly until smooth and instant-read thermometer inserted into mixture registers 71°C, about 15 minutes. Season to taste with salt and pepper. Remove hollandaise from over water; drape towel over bowl. Set aside while preparing fish. Cover saucepan with simmering water and keep over low heat.

Preheat oven to 149°C. Coat small rimmed baking sheet with 1 tablespoon butter. Sprinkle fish on both sides with coarse salt and pepper. Arrange fish on prepared baking sheet. Roast fish until just barely opaque in center, 10 to 12 minutes.
Meanwhile, melt remaining 2 tablespoons butter in medium nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add mushrooms; sprinkle with coarse salt and pepper and sauté until brown and tender, about 5 minutes.

Whisk 1 tablespoon of simmering water from saucepan into hollandaise. Place bowl with hollandaise over same saucepan with barely simmering water and whisk just until warm and smooth, adding more hot water by teaspoonfuls if sauce is very thick.
Divide sauce among 4 plates. Stack 2 fish fillets atop sauce on each. Top with mushrooms and serve.

tater-tot-casserole.name: Tater Tot Casserole
instructions: Sauté your garlic and onion in a splash of olive oil until soft. Add beef and brown thoroughly.
Place mixture in a casserole dish. To this, add all other ingredients except cheese and tater tots. Stir to combine.
Top with cheese, then top with tots. Spray with cooking spray.
Bake at 191°C for 25 minutes. Then, broil for 3-5 minutes or until brown.
Serve warm.

thai-tea-oreo-ice-cream.name: Thai Tea Oreo Ice Cream
instructions: Heat 1 cup half-and-half, heavy cream, and milk in a saucepan over medium heat until begins to simmer, about 10 minutes; stir in tea mix and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to low; simmer 5 to 10 minutes.

Beat egg in a bowl; whisk in about ¼ cup warm cream mixture, 1 teaspoon at a time. Whisk egg mixture back into cream mixture; cook until custard slightly thickened, about 1 minute. Strain custard through a cheesecloth-lined sieve into a bowl; discard tea leaves.

Beat remaining 1 cup half-and-half, sweetened condensed milk, and vanilla extract into custard until well blended; cover the bowl with plastic wrap and chill in the refrigerator, 6 hours to overnight.

Pour mixture into an ice cream maker and freeze according to manufacturer’s instructions, mixing in ¾ crushed cookies when semi-solid. Fold in remaining ¼ cookies when ice cream reaches desired consistency. Serve immediately or transfer to an airtight container and freeze until firm.

the-new-england-express-cocktail.name: The New England Express Cocktail
instructions: Bring sugar and 1/3 cup water to a boil in a small saucepan, stirring to dissolve sugar. Remove from heat, add thyme sprigs, and cover. Let stand 10 minutes, then strain into a small jar. Let cool.
DO AHEAD: Syrup can be made 1 month ahead. Cover and chill.
Mix thyme syrup, cider, rum, lime juice, and bitters in a pitcher. Divide among rocks glasses filled with ice; top off with club soda. Garnish with thyme sprigs and lime slices.
DO AHEAD: Thyme syrup, cider, rum, lime juice, and bitters can be mixed 4 hours ahead. Cover and chill.

the-picnic-pasta-salad.name: The Picnic Pasta Salad
instructions: Whisk Dijon mustard, cider vinegar, rice vinegar, salt, sugar, and cayenne together in a large bowl for about 30 seconds. Whisk in olive oil in 1-teaspoon increments until emulsified. Whisk in tarragon, dill, parsley, chives, and thyme. Remove and reserve 1/2 of the dressing in a separate small bowl.

Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil; cook pasta at a boil until tender yet firm to the bite, about 8 minutes. Add snow peas, carrots, peas, and broccoli about 2 minutes before the pasta is done. Drain pasta and veggies well, but do not rinse.

Transfer hot pasta mixture to the vinaigrette in the large mixing bowl and toss well to combine. Let rest for 5 minutes and toss again. Toss occasionally until the mixture is almost room temperature, 15 to 20 minutes.
Add bell peppers, cherry tomatoes, red onion, green onions, salt, and the reserved dressing and toss well.
You can serve this right away, but for best results, cover with plastic wrap and chill in the refrigerator for 4 to 6 hours. Toss and adjust seasoning before serving.

thracian-cheese-pie-argiro.name: Thracian Cheese Pie

ingredients.item: 1 kg of flour for all uses
2 cups fresh yeast or 2 sachets dry yeast
1 kg of flour for all uses
2 cups fresh yeast or 2 sachets dry yeast

instructions: First we activate the yeast we use. If you use dry yeast, you can put it directly into the materials.
In bowls, add the flour, yeast, sugar and mix.
Add water and knead until the dough is soft, supple and does not stick to the hands.

Place it in an oily bowl and cover it in a warm place to double for about 1 hour (depending on the temperature of the room). If we put her near the radiator, she'll swell fast if we just leave her on the counter, it'll take longer.
We put her on the counter and split her into four pieces.
We grease 32 or 34 cm.
Dip the hands into the olive oil with the fingers, spread the dough into a thick sheet into the oily baking pan, turning around and stretching it at the same time.
The diameter of the leaf should be as much as the diameter of the baking pan.
Sprinkle the feta cheese and oregano all over the surface.
We open the second sheet on the counter.
Put it on top.
That's like making four pieces on top of each other and between the filling.
The filling can be anything we like or each layer is different, another with feta and oregano, another with salami, another with olive oil and sun-dried tomato.
Repeat the same with all four cards.
After fixing everything and filling our liking in order we grab the edges and turn them into a ball.
Turn the ball over with the compounds touching the baking pan.
Click with the fingers on the surface to spread the shape and take the shape of the baking pan.
Brush the whole surface with oil and sprinkle sesame.
Cover and let it stand for 40′ in a warm place to swell.
Bake in a well preheated evening at 200oC or 180oC for about 40′ to brown and puff.
Warm is shocking.

tomato-and-garlic-pie.name: Tomato And Garlic Pie

ingredients.item: Stoned wheat thins or other whole grain crackers

instructions: Place a rack in middle of oven; preheat to 475°. Arrange tomatoes on a rimmed baking sheet, covering entire surface (it’s okay if they overlap). Drizzle with oil; season with salt and pepper. Roast until tomatoes begin to look dry on top, 25–30 minutes. Let cool.

Meanwhile, cook garlic and butter in a small saucepan over medium-low heat, swirling often, until butter foams and milk solids turn golden, 5–8 minutes. Strain through a fine-mesh sieve into a heatproof measuring cup; transfer garlic and toasty bits to a cutting board. Finely chop and set aside.

Reduce oven temperature to 375°. Pulse crackers and ¼ tsp. salt in a food processor until fine crumbs form (you should have about 2 cups). Add eggs and 6 Tbsp. garlic butter and pulse until mixture is the consistency of wet sand.Transfer to springform pan. Using a flat-sided measuring cup, press crumbs firmly onto bottom and 1½" up sides of pan. Bake crust until fragrant and edge is just starting to take on color, 8–10 minutes. Transfer pan to a wire rack and let crust cool.

Mix Taleggio, Parmesan, mayonnaise, chopped thyme, remaining ½ tsp. salt, remaining ¼ tsp. pepper, and reserved chopped garlic in a medium bowl. Gently dollop half of cheese mixture over bottom of crust, then spread into an even layer with an offset spatula (don’t press too hard or you will break the crust). Layer half of tomato slices over and press down to even out layers. Repeat with remaining cheese mixture and tomatoes. Brush remaining garlic butter over tomatoes. Top with shallot, then scatter thyme leaves over.
Bake pie until filling bubbles vigorously and crust is browned, 60–75 minutes. Let cool 1 hour before serving.
Do Ahead: Pie can be made 1 day ahead. Cover loosely and chill (it’s really good cold).

tomato-and-manchego-cheese-tartlets-tele.name: Tomato And Manchego Cheese Tartlets
tomato-and-manchego-cheese-tartlets-tele.original_name: Tarteletes de tomate e queijo manchego
instructions: Preheat the oven at 180 Unroll the masses, pick with a fork and cut in circles. Fill shapes for pielets, previously buttered.
Corte os tomates em rodelas. Coloque no fundo de cada forma um pouco de mostarda e depois uma ou duas rodelas de tomate. Polvilhe com o queijo, regue com um fio de azeite e tempere com sal e pimenta.
Disponha as formas num tabuleiro e leve ao forno durante cerca de 25 a 30 minutos. Retire, deixe arrefecer, desenforme e sirva polvilhadas com cebolinho picado.

tomato-mozzarella-salad.name: Tomato Mozzarella Salad
instructions: Place tomato slices, alternating with mozzarella slices, on a large serving platter.
Combine oil, balsamic vinegar, salt, and pepper in a jar with a tight-fitting lid; shake well.
Drizzle over tomatoes and mozzarella; sprinkle with basil.
Enjoy!

tomato-omelette-pequerecetas.name: Tomato Omelette

ingredients.item: 1 cebolla roja (o cualquier otra que no pique)
Several tablespoons of flour (variable quantity, depending on the liquid that the tomatoes release)

instructions: We cut the tomatoes and onion into dadits, and we cut the parsley. We put into a bowl and we pat. We add the scrambled eggs and several tablespoons of flour (depending on the liquid that have been released from the tomatoes) that are sufficient to make the mixture's fluid not too light but slightly sticky to hold the ingredients together.
On a large frying pan or plate, we put an oil apple and we put out spoons of dough to make the pancakes. When they're put up with them, we turn them around.
Servimos con salsa de yogur.

ton-negima-grilled-pork-belly-and-scalli.name: Ton Negima (grilled Pork Belly And Scallion Skewers)
instructions: Thread the skewers by alternating the pork belly and scallion (about 3 pieces of pork belly and 2 pieces of scallion per skewer) so the pieces cover about 4 inches of skewer. As you work, turn the skewers in a screwing motion to make it easier to pierce the pork and scallion. When the skewers are ready, gently press down on each one with the heel of your hand to compact the meat. Lightly season all sides with salt.

Preheat a grill to medium-hot. Grill skewers for 5 to 6 minutes, turning every minute to brown evenly. Be careful not to burn the pork belly. Don’t hesitate to spray flare-ups with water, or shift skewers as you grill, especially if you notice some skewers browning too fast over a hot spot; trade them with skewers grilling over a less hot part. Serve immediately. Accent with sansho.

torta-di-pane-al-cioccolato.name: Torta Di Pane Al Cioccolato

ingredients.item: Nonstick vegetable oil spray or unsalted butter
"-thick slices dried-out sourdough or country-style bread
Diamond crystal or  0.25 tsp. morton kosher salt

ingredients.prep: coarsely chopped, or semisweet chocolate chips

instructions: Bring cream, star anise, and aniseed (if using) to a boil over medium heat in a small saucepan. Remove from heat, cover, and let sit at least 30 minutes and up to 2 hours.
Strain cream through a fine-mesh sieve into a measuring glass, pressing on solids to extract as much cream as possible; discard solids. Add more cream if needed to bring the amount back up to ¾ cup.

Place a rack in middle of oven; preheat to 350°. Line bottom of an 8"-diameter cake pan with a parchment paper round; spray sides of pan with nonstick spray or lightly coat with butter. Pulse or blend bread in a food processor or blender until finely ground.

Using an electric mixer on high speed, beat eggs, sugar, and salt in a large bowl until pale and fluffy, about 4 minutes. With motor running, gradually add cream. Once cream is combined, add finely ground bread and baking powder; mix well. Using a rubber spatula, fold in biscuits and chocolate. Scrape batter into prepared pan and rap pan on counter.

Bake cake until golden and top springs back when lightly pressed, 25–30 minutes. Transfer pan to a wire rack and let cake cool in pan 5 minutes. Invert cake onto rack and unmold. Let cool completely. (You can also eat the torta warm while the chocolate is still in molten pools or chill and crumble it into a cup of yogurt or milk.)

Peel parchment paper away from cake; discard. Place cake, right side up, on a platter. Cut into wedges and serve with whipped cream, dusted with cocoa powder if desired.
Do ahead: Cake can be baked 1 day ahead. Store airtight at room temperature.

tortellini-primavera.name: Tortellini Primavera
instructions: Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook tortellini in boiling water until tender yet firm to the bite and filling is warmed through, 5 to 7 minutes; drain.

Combine cooked tortellini, tomatoes, broccoli, yellow bell pepper, Parmesan cheese, and pepperoni in a large serving bowl. Pour in 1 cup Italian dressing and toss to coat. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 4 hours. If salad looks dry, stir in remaining 1/4 cup Italian dressing before serving.

traditional-cupcakes-teleculinaria.name: Traditional Cupcakes
instructions: Garnish for muffins with butter and sprinkle them with flour. Start the oven at 180 degrees.

In a bowl, beat the butter well with the sugar and some lemon zest. Then add the eggs, one by one and always beating very well. Add warm milk, always beating. Then add the flour, previously mixed with the yeast, and beat again.
Divide the previous mixture through the tins and put it in the oven for 25 minutes. Make sure the cupcakes are well cooked, remove them from the oven, unfurl them, let them cool and serve.

traditional-eggplant-pie-argiro.name: Traditional Eggplant Pie
instructions: Mix all the ingredients together and add the water a little bit until the dough becomes firm.
Form on a stick and cut it into 8 pieces.
Place them in a floured bowl, to rest.

turkey-pesto-croffle-sandwiches.name: Turkey Pesto Croffle Sandwiches

ingredients.item: Shredded colby jack or mozzarella cheese

instructions: Preheat a nonstick waffle maker to medium-high heat. Lightly grease with butter or cooking spray.
Place 1 thawed croissant on the center of the waffle maker. Close the lid gently but firmly, and cook for 3–4 minutes, until golden, crispy, and cooked through. Repeat with the second croissant.
Transfer croffles to a cutting board, and cut each in half horizontally with a serrated knife.

Spread ½ teaspoon of pesto on each half of the croffle (1 teaspoon total per sandwich). Top each with 1 Colby cheese slice, ¼ cup roasted bell peppers, 2 turkey slices, 1 provolone cheese slice, and 2 tablespoons shredded Colby Jack cheese. Top with the remaining croffle halves.

Place 1 sandwich back on the waffle maker. Press the lid down gently, and cook for 1–2 minutes, until the cheese is melted and the filling is warmed through. Repeat with the second sandwich. Serve hot.
Enjoy!

turkey-salad.name: Turkey Salad
instructions: Place cooked turkey, celery, green onions, and red bell pepper in a blender or food processor. Pulse until finely chopped.

Transfer turkey mixture to a medium bowl. Mix in mayonnaise, prepared Dijon-style mustard, cider vinegar, white sugar, and salt until well combined. Cover and refrigerate, 8 hours to overnight, before serving.

turkey-stuffing-argiro.name: Turkey Stuffing

ingredients.item: 1/2 tablespoon orange zest and 1 orange juice
1/2 tablespoon orange zest and 1 orange juice

instructions: Brush the bread chops and bake them in a preheated oven at 200°C for 6–7 minutes, until golden. Allow them to cool a little.
Clean the membranes from the intestines, wash them well and chop them.
In a large pan or pot, heat the olive oil. Add the minced meat, bacon and chopped off. Sauté over high heat for 6–7 minutes until golden.
Add the onion chopped and continue to sauté for 4–5 minutes until soft.
Remove the mixture with the cognac, mix well and allow the alcohol to evaporate.
Add the orange juice and allow the mixture to glaze, gently stirring.
Add the chopped plums, chestnuts, chopped bread frames, apples, nuts, pine cones, orange zest and salt and pepper.
Mix all the ingredients well, remove from heat and allow the mixture to cool just before using it for filling in the turkey.

turmeric-ginger-tonic-with-chia-seeds.name: Turmeric-ginger Tonic With Chia Seeds

ingredients.prep: coarsely chopped, or  0.5 teaspoon dried turmeric

instructions: Blend ginger, turmeric, honey, lime juice, black peppercorns, and 3 cups water in a blender until smooth. Strain through a fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl. Stir in chia seeds and let sit until seeds begin to swell, 8–10 minutes.
Divide tonic among ice-filled glasses. Top off with club soda and garnish with thyme sprigs, if desired.
Do Ahead: Tonic can be made 1 day ahead. Cover and chill. Stir vigorously before topping off with club soda.

turnip-green-soup.name: Turnip Green Soup

ingredients.item: Salt and ground black pepper to taste

instructions: Heat oil in a large saucepan over medium heat. Add kielbasa and onion; cook and stir until kielbasa is browned and onion is translucent, 5 to 7 minutes. Remove to a plate.
Add broth, turnip greens, beans, soup mix, hot pepper sauce, garlic powder, Creole seasoning, salt, and pepper to the saucepan; bring to a boil over medium-high heat.
Return kielbasa and onion mixture to the saucepan. Reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 2 hours.
